title: lichen_link_tx.c:250 CRC-disabled error path skips stack buffer wipe
description:
Trigger: lichen_link_tx() is called with no link key (ctx->has_link_key false) while CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_INSECURE_CRC32_MIC is not enabled. The function has already compressed the IPv6 packet into compressed[] at link/lichen_link_tx.c:79-84 and copied it into payload_buf at link/lichen_link_tx.c:103-135, but the CRC-disabled branch returns -EINVAL directly at link/lichen_link_tx.c:245-250 instead of going through the cleanup label at link/lichen_link_tx.c:257-263.

Impact: compressed packet contents and any generated signed payload remain in stack storage despite the explicit SECURITY cleanup comment. This is not a packet-format failure, but it undermines the module's documented secret/data hygiene and creates a branch contributors can copy when adding new error paths.

Relevant refs: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_tx.c:35, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_tx.c:80, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_tx.c:110, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_tx.c:245, lichen/subsys/lichen/link/lichen_link_tx.c:257.
close_reason: Fixed CRC-disabled/no-key TX error path to use cleanup and wipe stack buffers
